name: Geraint
description: Geraint mab Erbin, also known as Geraint of Dumnonia, is a celebrated hero from Welsh Arthurian tradition. He appears prominently in the Mabinogion tale 'Geraint and Enid,' where he is depicted as a noble and valiant knight of King Arthur's court. Geraint's story revolves around themes of love, honor, jealousy, and redemption. Initially, he is portrayed as a skilled warrior and a loyal servant to Arthur, but his marriage to Enid leads to a period of intense jealousy and mistrust. He misinterprets Enid's genuine concern for his well-being as infidelity, causing him to embark on a series of perilous adventures to test her loyalty and his own strength. Throughout these trials, Geraint faces numerous enemies, including giants, knights, and treacherous earls, showcasing his exceptional combat prowess. Eventually, he realizes his error in doubting Enid and learns to trust her completely. Their relationship is restored, solidifying their bond and reaffirming Geraint's status as a hero. Geraint's tale is a complex exploration of human flaws and the importance of communication and understanding in relationships. His character represents the ideals of chivalry, bravery, and ultimately, forgiveness and self-improvement. He is a significant figure in Welsh Arthurian lore, exemplifying both the strengths and weaknesses of heroic figures.
subtitle: Knight of Arthur, Tested by Love
achievements: Defeated the Knight of the Sparrowhawk

keyMyths.title: Geraint and Enid
keyMyths.description: Geraint, a knight of Arthur, marries Enid, but his jealousy leads him to test her loyalty through dangerous adventures. They eventually reconcile, reaffirming their love and trust.

keyMyths.title: The Tournament at Caerleon
keyMyths.description: Geraint proves his valor and skill in combat by winning the tournament held at Arthur's court in Caerleon. This solidifies his reputation as a formidable warrior.

keyMyths.title: The Knight of the Sparrowhawk
keyMyths.description: Geraint defeats the arrogant Knight of the Sparrowhawk, avenging the insult to Gwenhwyfar and proving his strength and chivalry.

associations.name: Enid

Shares warrior ethos and superhuman combat abilities.
Embodies courage and faces difficult challenges, though Sigurd's are more overtly supernatural.

worshipPractices.name: None documented
worshipPractices.description: Geraint is a literary figure rather than a deity, so there were no specific worship practices associated with him. He was celebrated in stories and poems.

cultural.modernLegacy: Geraint continues to inspire modern literature, art, and film, representing the ideals of chivalry and the complexities of human relationships. His tale is retold and reinterpreted, keeping the character relevant.

extendedContent.title: Early Life and Lineage
extendedContent.content: Geraint, son of Erbin, hailed from Dumnonia, a kingdom in what is now southwestern England. His lineage connected him to a region with a rich history and cultural identity, influencing his sense of duty and honor. Though details of his early life are scarce, it's presumed he received training befitting a noble, focusing on combat, horsemanship, and courtly manners. His upbringing instilled in him a strong sense of justice and a commitment to protecting the vulnerable. This foundation prepared him for his later role as a knight in King Arthur's court, where he would become known for his valor and skill. Geraint's origins in Dumnonia also gave him a unique perspective, bridging Welsh and Brythonic traditions, enriching the Arthurian legend with diverse cultural influences.

extendedContent.title: Greatest Quests and Trials
extendedContent.content: Geraint's most famous quest is recounted in 'Geraint and Enid.' Initially a celebrated knight, his reputation suffers due to his perceived idleness after marriage. Driven by jealousy and a desire to prove his worth, he embarks on a series of adventures, often testing his wife Enid's loyalty and his own abilities. He battles formidable knights, rescues damsels in distress, and confronts treacherous enemies, demonstrating his martial prowess and unwavering courage. These trials serve as a crucible, refining his character and teaching him the importance of trust and communication in relationships. The quest culminates in a reconciliation with Enid and the restoration of his honor, proving that true strength lies not only in battle but also in self-awareness and humility. His journey showcases the Arthurian ideals of chivalry, loyalty, and redemption.

extendedContent.title: Legacy and Enduring Influence
extendedContent.content: Geraint's legacy extends beyond the medieval tales, influencing modern interpretations of Arthurian legend. He represents the complexities of heroism, demonstrating that even the most valiant knights are susceptible to human flaws. His story explores themes of love, jealousy, and reconciliation, resonating with audiences across centuries. Geraint's character serves as a reminder that true strength lies not only in physical prowess but also in emotional intelligence and the ability to learn from one's mistakes. He continues to be a source of inspiration for writers, artists, and filmmakers, who reimagine his story in various forms. His enduring presence in popular culture ensures that the values of chivalry, courage, and the importance of relationships remain relevant in contemporary society. Geraint stands as a testament to the enduring power of myth and its ability to reflect and shape human values.

shortDescription: A valiant knight of Arthurian legend known for his martial skill and the tale of love and redemption with his wife, Enid. He embodies chivalry and courage.
